description: When something is impossible, assuming it leads straight to disaster.

guides.text: The goal is $\lnot A \to (A \to \bot)$. In words: if $\lnot A$ is true, then supposing $A$ lands you in a contradiction ($\bot$, pronounced 'bottom' or 'falsity'). This is what negation **means** in our system — $\lnot A$ is the claim that $A$ implies falsity.
As always with an implication, assume the left-hand side. This time it's $\lnot A$ — a compound formula we haven't worked with before. The engine parses `(not A)` as $\lnot A$.
The rule `not-elim` converts a negation into the implication it stands for. From $\lnot A$ at step 1, it produces $A \to \bot$ — '$A$ implies falsity'. The context stays the same.
We now have $A \to \bot$ under the assumption $\lnot A$. Use `->-intro` to discharge and finish.

unlocks.name: ¬-elim
unlocks.description: From $\lnot p$ derive $p \to \bot$ — negation unwraps into an implication to falsity.
